title: DDE Links
text: DDE Links Applications that communiicate via DDE do so using links that can be defined by copying and pasting them to the destination application or programming them using the DDE syntax rules. Most applications have a Paste Link or Paste Special option that can be used to to copy the required data from the source application to the Microsoft Windows clipboard, and then paste it into the destination application. When the data is modified in the source application, it is also updated in the destination application. Uniface does not offer a Paste Link command, so using a Uniface application as a DDE client requires some ProcScript programming. However, Uniface does accept pasted links when acting as a DDE server. Related Links Uniface as a DDE Client Uniface as a DDE Server When Uniface acts as a DDE server, the DDE link must be defined in the client application. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
